title: Neotectonic deformation of the central Chile margin: Deepwater forearc basin formation in response to hot spot ridge and seamount subduction
abstract: The Valparaiso Basin is a deep-water forearc basin underlying a 40×60 km mid-slope terrace at 2.5 km water depth on the central Chile margin. Seismic-reflection data from the CONDOR investigation image a 3–3.5 km thick sediment succession. In response to underthrusting of the Juan Fernández Ridge, the basin fill is deformed above seaward-vergent outer forearc compressional highs. Basal erosion enhanced by JFR subduction thinned the continental crust and increased the subsidence rate to form the Valparaíso Forearc Basin.

location.description: Valparaíso deep-water forearc basin offshore central Chile (~32°–33°S); Juan Fernández Ridge subduction zone

seismic_lines.name: CONDOR high-resolution seismic reflection grid — Valparaíso corridor ~32°–34°S, 2000 line-km, 10–15 km spacing (5–8 km in Valparaíso Basin)

data.name: CONDOR MCS Profiles — Valparaíso Forearc Basin
data.description: MCS reflection profiles from CONDOR experiment imaging 3–3.5 km sediment succession in Valparaíso deep-water forearc basin; deformation by seaward-vergent compressional highs from JFR underthrusting; basin dimensions 40×60 km at 2.5 km depth

_data_note: Tectonics paper; behind paywall (accessed via Cochrane Chile). High-resolution seismic data from CONDOR SO101, 1995. Not open access. Data repository unconfirmed.

processing.summary: MCS reflection data from the CONDOR investigation (SO101, 1993) were processed and interpreted to characterize the Valparaíso deep-water forearc basin. The profiles image 3–3.5 km of sediment fill deformed above compressional highs formed by JFR underthrusting. Prestack and poststack processing reveals basal erosion and crustal thinning responsible for basin formation and subsidence.
workflow: 5. NMO correction and stacking (simple 3-layer velocity model)
7. Dip-filter in f-k domain (CMP bin expansion 3.125→6.25 m)
processing.notes: CONDOR SO101, 1995. 24-channel 150m Teledyne analogue streamer, 160 cui sleeve-gun (4 guns), 1 ms sampling. Migration assisted by D. Klaesch and C.R. Ranero. Companion to von Huene et al. 1997 Tectonics paper.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Behind paywall (accessed via Cochrane Chile). 3 authors confirmed: Laursen J., Scholl D.W., von Huene Roland. DOI confirmed. Vessel: 'R/V SONNE' → 'R/V SONNE (SO101, CONDOR, 1995)' (from acknowledgments + ref 'Cruise Rep. SO101-CONDOR'). year_acquired: 1993 → 1995 (paper states 'acquired in 1995'). source_volume_cui: null → 160 (4 sleeve guns, 160 cui). streamer_length_m: null → 150 (24-channel 150m Teledyne analogue). channel_count: null → 24. sample_rate_ms: 4 → 1 (1 ms stated in paper). line_spacing_km: null → 12 (10–15 km average). bounding_box expanded to 32°–34°S. Processing workflow updated from Section 3. No explicit software cited.
